accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurate context; trustee comp, fee distributions, and judgeship extensions are correctly described.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradicting claims; the $120 total and $45-to-$105 increase match the bill's findings and amendments.

states_purpose.why: Opening clearly identifies the bill's purpose: increase trustee compensation, adjust fees, and extend judgeship terms.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly names amended statutes including Section 330 of Title 11, Section 589a of Title 28, and the 2020 and 2017 judgeship acts.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes effective-date exceptions for trustee compensation and bankruptcy fees, capturing the material carve-outs.

effective_dates.why: Accurately describes the general effective date and the chapter 7 October 1 and chapter 11 quarter timing provisions.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all essential operative effects across Sections 3, 4, and 5 without misrepresentation.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive tone throughout with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized by section, internally consistent, and clearly written.

concise.why: Efficient with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: All named statutes and funds are correctly identified, including the Deficit Reduction Act of 2005 fund and US Trustee System Fund.

correct_figures.why: Figures ($45 to $105, $120 total, $5.4 million, 5 to 10 years, 2026 to 2031) all match the bill text.
